text.title: Generative UI Design Workflow
text.summary: Tool comparison, five-stage AI design workflow, and quality rules for generative UI output.

list.item: Galileo — UI generation, production-ready visual quality, no public API (UI-only)
Uizard — wireframe-to-design rapid prototypes, project API in beta
v0 (Vercel) — React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui code output, CLI available
Claude Artifacts — interactive prototypes, most agent-native path
Relume — wireframe generation from sitemap, Figma-compatible export
Ideation — AI generates 3–5 layout variants from a written spec
Selection — human curates best variant based on design goals
Refinement — agent + human iterate on the selected variant
Brand alignment — human verifies against brand guidelines
Implementation — AI-assisted coding with accessibility audit before handoff

rule.statement: Generate 3–5 variants per screen, not 1 — comparison is faster than iteration from a single bad start.
rule.rationale: AI-generated layouts are often visually generic. Comparing multiple variants simultaneously exposes the design space faster than iterating on one output.

rule.statement: Always start with a written spec before prompting; vague prompts produce generic output.
rule.rationale: "Generate a dashboard" produces unusable output. "Generate a SaaS analytics dashboard with date-range filter, KPI cards, and a line chart" produces useful starting points.

rule.statement: Run axe-cli on any generated prototype before stakeholder review.
rule.rationale: No generative UI tool produces accessible keyboard navigation, skip-to-content, or screen-reader-friendly markup by default. Presenting inaccessible UIs as viable designs creates false expectations.

rule.statement: Treat generated code as a sketch, not a PR — always refactor before merging into a real codebase.
rule.rationale: Generated component names often conflict with existing project components. Generated code references props or methods that may not exist in the actual library version the project uses.

antipattern.description: Using v0 output when the project does not use Tailwind + shadcn/ui.
antipattern.reason: v0 generates Tailwind + shadcn/ui exclusively. If the project uses a different stack, the generated code requires full rewrite of component imports and styling.

antipattern.description: Auto-deploying generated UI code to production without designer and developer review.
antipattern.reason: Generated code is prototype-grade. It consistently misses accessibility requirements, may conflict with existing component naming, and uses non-deterministic output that cannot be relied upon for production.
